description_long: This object is a compact, double-barreled shotgun with a short, robust design, featuring a dark metallic finish on the barrels and a wooden grip. The barrels are arranged side-by-side, giving the weapon a broad, imposing front profile. The grip is curved and made of wood, contrasting with the sleek, black metal of the barrels and receiver. The trigger guard is rounded and encloses two triggers, indicating the ability to fire each barrel independently. The overall construction appears solid and functional, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing utility. This type of shotgun is often associated with close-quarters use due to its short barrel length and lack of a full stock.
description: A compact, side-by-side double-barreled shotgun with a dark metal finish and a curved wooden grip. The design is robust and utilitarian, suited for close-range use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin barrels side-by-side, with the dark metal finish and the rounded muzzle ends clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the short, double-barrel configuration, the curved wooden grip, and the rounded trigger guard.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the wooden grip and the back of the receiver, with the twin barrels aligned closely together.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with the wooden grip and metal barrels, but from the other side.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, short barrel length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with smooth edges and no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
